name: LLM request queueing + batching policies
description: Production LLM serving should use explicit queueing/batching with backpressure and scheduling to manage the throughput vs latency tradeoff and protect tail latencies. Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/optimize-llm

name: Embedding quantization (binary/int8) + rescoring
description: Post-process embeddings to binary or int8 to cut storage (binary up to 32×) and accelerate retrieval (reported up to 32×), then rescore candidates to retain relevance (~96% retention reported with rescoring). Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/embedding-quantization

name: Standardized model risk scoring (RiskRubric.ai)
description: Use standardized risk evaluations with pillar scores (transparency, reliability, security, privacy, safety, reputation) and automated test/probe counts (1,000+ reliability tests; 200+ adversarial probes) to support model selection and governance. Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/riskrubric

name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool servers for agents
description: Adopt MCP-compatible tool servers to standardize tool integration across agent providers; Microsoft Agent Framework highlights MCP server integration via GitHub Copilot SDK. Source: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/build-ai-agents-with-github-copilot-sdk-and-microsoft-agent-framework/

name: Hybrid dense + knowledge-graph RAG stacks (LightRAG-style)
description: Packaged retrieval subsystem combining vector index + lightweight knowledge graph, hybrid retrieval, optional reranking, and integrations for RAGAS evaluation and Langfuse tracing (deployable via Docker). Source: knowledge base node 'HKUDS LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation' (no URL stored).

name: Arena-style community benchmarking for privacy guardrails
description: Use blind, adversarial community testing (Elo-ranked leaderboard) to compare privacy guardrails across guardrailed LLM configurations. Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/arena-lighthouz

name: Workflow-aware safety/security guard models (AprielGuard)
description: Guard model designed to classify safety risks and detect adversarial attacks across prompts, multi-turn conversations, and agentic workflows (including tool calls and reasoning traces). Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/ServiceNow-AI/aprielguard

name: Single-turn, narrow-scope safety classifiers
description: Traditional safety classifiers that assume short inputs and single user messages are misaligned with multi-turn, long-context, tool-using agent deployments. Source: https://huggingface.co/blog/ServiceNow-AI/aprielguard
